era: 1994-1995 original; 2011 reunion. Grunge. E=7 -- elevated from E=6 at degree 224. Grunge frame foregrounds physical weight. Capitol Hill. Columbia Records.
key_album: Above (1995, Columbia) -- one album, complete. River of Deceit. Billboard 200 #24. 250,000+ US copies.

theory_nodes.concept: Grunge frame at return. E=6 Rock (224) to E=7 Grunge (305). The return foregrounds physical weight: Lifeless Dead, the density of Staley's voice, the scene's mortality.
theory_nodes.description: Energy escalates at return: Rock to Grunge, E=6 to E=7.

theory_nodes.concept: Layne Staley death April 5 2002. Eight years after Cobain. Combined cocaine-heroin overdose. Age 34. Found two weeks after death. The most important loss from the Seattle scene.
theory_nodes.description: Staley's death as Seattle scene's defining loss.

theory_nodes.concept: 'River of Deceit' as permanent standard. Self-chosen pain. Resignation and transcendence. The scene's most direct engagement with mortality. 30-year critical reassessment elevates it continuously.
theory_nodes.description: The song that made the scene's relationship with addiction permanent in music history.

theory_nodes.concept: 2011 reunion with Mark Lanegan. Above survives the loss of its primary voice. Lanegan inhabits Staley's material without imitation.
theory_nodes.description: Screaming Trees connection: Martin in both bands; Lanegan sings for Staley at reunion.

cross_references.relationship: First appearance E=6. Rock frame. The supergroup story: lineup, recovery narrative, single-album achievement. 81-degree gap to return.
Andrew Wood's death. The first scene death. Mad Season (Staley, Saunders) continues the mortality thread.
Mark Lanegan sang for Staley at 2011 reunion. Barrett Martin is the Screaming Trees-Mad Season bridge.

sps_pair.pairing_note: Both E=7. Confrontational display: bear asserts territory via wood-crack + huff; Mad Season asserts emotional weight via Staley's voice at maximum.
